task: feat: add entry-not-touched diagnostics (1순위 작업, MONE_AGNAS_remaining_work_plan.txt section 4)

causeCandidatesTop.summary: Entry-not-touched trades have a meaningfully shorter avg entry_window_days (4.1d) than entry-touched trades (5.95d) -- about 31% shorter.

Entry-not-touched trades show higher avg 5-day momentum at signal time (10.66 vs 5.38) and a high MA-full-alignment rate (90%), consistent with the hypothesis that some strong-trend candidates ran away before pulling back to the recommended entry.
US market entry-not-touched rate (2.37%) is about 6x the KR rate (0.41%).
Aggressive mode entry-not-touched rate (2.42%) is about 6x the conservative rate (0.42%).

notSupportedHypothesis.ENTRY_PRICE_TOO_CONSERVATIVE: Avg entry depth for entry-not-touched trades (0.075% below signal price) is barely different from the entry-touched baseline (0.002%). The data does NOT support 'entry price set too deep/conservative' as the primary cause, contrary to the generic suggestion already surfaced in trade_improvement_priorities.py ('진입가가 지나치게 보수적인지 점검 필요'). Do not shorten/raise entry depth based on that generic suggestion alone -- this snapshot is more specific evidence against it.

interpretation: Sample size is small (20 evaluated entry-not-touched trades out of 1445), so directional signals here are suggestive, not conclusive. The two most plausible mechanisms are (1) entry_window_days being too short for some recommendations to ever fill, and (2) some candidates already showing strong momentum/MA-alignment at signal time continuing to run away rather than pulling back to the entry price -- i.e. the opposite of the 'entry too conservative' hypothesis. Both are consistent with the work plan's caution: classify causes before touching the entry price formula.
recommendedNextStep: Do not change the entry price formula yet (sample too small, primary hypothesis not supported). If entryNotTouchedTrades grows past ~40, re-run this diagnostic and consider: (a) a shallower-pullback or time-decaying entry option for high-momentum/MA-aligned candidates specifically, (b) reviewing whether entry_window_days is set appropriately per horizon, especially for US/aggressive where the rate is highest.
